description_long: This is a mid-rise, rectangular brick hostel building with five stories, featuring a flat roof equipped with rooftop utilities such as antennas and HVAC units. The facade is primarily red brick, with some weathering and graffiti on the lower levels, and a fire escape ladder and staircase running along one side. The ground floor includes a commercial-style entrance with large windows and signage, while the upper floors have regularly spaced, rectangular windows, some of which are partially boarded or covered. The rear and one side of the building are mostly plain brick, with minimal window openings and no decorative elements. The building sits on a dark, paved base, suggesting an urban sidewalk or street context. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with elements typical of early-to-mid 20th-century urban architecture.
description: A five-story red brick hostel with a flat roof, fire escape, and ground-floor commercial entrance, featuring weathered facades and urban graffiti.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance, ground-floor signage, and four stories of windows above, with rooftop utilities visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the fire escape ladder and staircase, additional windows, and more graffiti on the lower wall.
description_view_2: Opposite side view reveals a plainer brick wall with fewer windows and more graffiti near the base.
description_view_3: Rear view displays a large, uninterrupted brick wall with minimal features except for rooftop antennas.
attribute_signage: The front facade above the entrance features a rectangular sign with the text 'WOKA WOKA' in large, uppercase letters. The sign is faded, with a beige background and dark lettering, and is accompanied by graffiti and smaller tags on the lower wall.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance is located at the center of the front facade, consisting of a wide, rectangular doorway flanked by large glass windows. The entrance is framed by concrete or stone, with a recessed doorway and a transom window above. There are no visible steps or ramps, and the entry is at street level.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular grid pattern on the front and side facades, with four windows per floor on the front and five on the side. All windows are rectangular, some with black or white frames, and a few are partially boarded or covered. The rear facade has no windows, while the opposite side has a sparse arrangement.
attribute_architectural_style: Utilitarian early-to-mid 20th-century urban style, with minimal ornamentation and functional features such as fire escapes and flat roof.
